expectations.text: Uses event { handler { ... } } wrapper (not events) for single-shot response
expectations.evidence: Line 12: `Method.GET / "" -> event { handler {...} }` and line 18: `Method.GET / "greet" -> event { handler {...} }` - correct wrapper

expectations.text: Handler returns DatastarEvent.patchElements(...) directly (not a ZIO effect)
expectations.evidence: Lines 13-15 and 19-26: Both handlers return `DatastarEvent.patchElements(...)` as values, not wrapped in ZIO

expectations.text: Uses dataOn.submit attribute for form submission without page reload
expectations.evidence: Line 47: `dataOn.submit := js"@get('/greet')"` - correct Datastar attribute for form submission

expectations.text: Includes datastarScript in the HTML head
Reads form input (name) from request and personalizes the greeting
expectations.evidence: Line 20: `val name = req.queryParam("name").getOrElse("Guest")` - correctly reads name from query params and personalizes greeting at line 24

expectations.text: Serves the initial HTML page (with form visible initially, greeting hidden until submitted)
expectations.evidence: Lines 12-15: First route serves initial page; line 69: `div(id("greeting"), className := "greeting-container")` - greeting div initially empty and hidden via CSS at line 167

notes: Excellent implementation. Follows Step 4 (Single-Shot Response) exactly from the skill. Clean use of event wrapper, returns DatastarEvent values directly, uses proper form submission via dataOn.submit. Code is correct and would compile without issues.
